checks.name: tenant and repository authority
checks.summary: Composite keys, scoped queries, operator-bound storage configuration, and cross-tenant negative tests prevent callers from selecting or crossing canonical authorities.

checks.name: object integrity and substitution resistance
checks.summary: Keys derive only from validated authority and SHA-256, conditional writes reject replacement, first publication is fully reread, and purge requires exact ETag plus provider version evidence.

checks.name: memory privilege separation
checks.summary: memory:promote is excluded from broad permission sets, untrusted agents and workers are mechanically denied it, and policy promotion requires the exact configured authenticated system principal.

checks.name: conversation and citation provenance
checks.summary: Immutable messages reference active tenant-scoped artifacts, and conversation closure recomputes canonical transcript bytes plus the exact expected source-reference set.

checks.name: retention and failure recovery
checks.summary: Tombstones commit before deletion, every live canonical reference is rechecked under lock, terminal reconciliation conflicts persist, and receipt verification rejects orphan knowledge events.

residual_risks: PostgreSQL DBA, host root, CI administrator, and configured object-store administrator are trusted deployment roles and can alter their respective planes outside application controls.
Provider-specific conditional-write and checksum behavior must pass the documented capability probe before production admission.
Object-store credentials, network policy, encryption keys, bucket retention, and disaster-recovery geography remain deployment responsibilities.
Active-memory listing is bounded but not yet batched; this is a performance risk rather than an authorization or integrity risk.
Compiler lineage, semantic retrieval, graph projection, and context assembly remain intentionally absent until Sprints 08 through 11.
